◐ I'm settling on a concrete approach: strip out the layout query parameter and its 
  associated CSS branches, migrate the grid system to native CSS Grid with custom 
  properties for column and row counts, then calculate optimal dimensions by measuring 
  the component's aspect ratio and determining how many rows and columns best fit the 
  export count.

  The key insight is that the grid aspect ratio should be the inverse of the component 
  aspect ratio—so if a component is tall (high aspect ratio), the grid should be wide 
  (low aspect ratio). I'll enumerate
